quiz_title: Mapei Ceramic Line — Product Knowledge Quiz
source: MAPEI_PRODUCT_KNOWLEDGE.xlsx — built from official Mapei TDSs
design_notes: All distractors are real values pulled from other Mapei products in the same data column, so wrong answers are plausible — not obvious.

questions.question: Which Mapei product is a two-component adhesive that uses a latex liquid instead of water?
questions.explanation: Granirapid is a two-component system — Part A powder is mixed directly into Part B latex; no water is added.

questions.question: What colour options does Adesilex P7 come in?
questions.explanation: Adesilex P7 is the only standard cementitious adhesive in the Mapei ceramic line offered in grey only — no white version.

questions.question: What is the bag size of the Ultralite S2 adhesive?
questions.explanation: All three Ultralite products (S1, S2, S2 Quick) come in 15 kg lightweight bags — same volume as a conventional 25 kg bag.

questions.question: Keracolor FF is which type of product?
questions.explanation: Keracolor FF is a polymer-modified cementitious grout (CG2WA) for joints up to 6 mm — it fills tile joints, it does not bond tiles.

questions.question: Mapesil AC is supplied in what packaging?
questions.explanation: Mapesil AC is a one-component silicone sealant supplied in 310 ml cartridges — applied with a standard caulking gun.

questions.question: How many colours is Mapesil AC available in (excluding transparent)?
questions.explanation: Mapesil AC is available in 40 colours plus transparent — the largest colour palette in the entire Mapei ceramic line.

questions.question: Keralastic and Keralastic T are what kind of adhesive?
questions.explanation: Both are two-component reaction-resin (R2 / R2T) polyurethane adhesives — no water, no cement, classified per EN 12004 as R2.

questions.question: Kerapoxy is a two-component epoxy product. What does it function as?
questions.explanation: Kerapoxy is dual-classified — RG (reaction-resin grout per EN 13888) and R2T (reaction-resin adhesive per EN 12004). It can be used as either.

questions.question: Mapesil Z Plus is specifically formulated for which application area?
questions.explanation: Mapesil Z Plus is a mould-resistant acetic silicone designed for bathrooms, showers, saunas, kitchens — sanitary fittings. Not for pool expansion joints (use Mapesil AC for those).

questions.question: What does the letter 'F' stand for in a Mapei adhesive classification code (e.g. C2F)?
questions.explanation: F = fast-setting / rapid-hardening per ISO 13007 / EN 12004. Granirapid (C2F S1) and Ultralite S2 Quick (C2FE S2) carry the F.

questions.question: What is the pot life of Granirapid once mixed?
questions.explanation: Granirapid is fast-setting — pot life 45 min. The other options are real pot lives of other Mapei products (Mapesil skin time, Keracolor FF, standard C2 adhesives).

questions.question: What does the 'S2' classification mean in C2E S2?
questions.explanation: S2 = highly deformable per EN 12004 (transversal deformation ≥ 5 mm). S1 = deformable (≥ 2.5 mm and < 5 mm). T (not S) means slip-resistant.

questions.question: How long after installation can a swimming pool tiled with Granirapid be filled with water?
questions.explanation: Granirapid is fast-setting — pools fillable after 3 days. Most standard cementitious adhesives need 21 days; Kerapoxy needs 10; Keracolor FF as grout needs 7.

questions.question: What is the open time of Adesilex P7?
questions.explanation: Adesilex P7 has NO 'E' in its code (just C2T) — open time only 20 min. The 'E' adhesives (Keraflex, Kerabond Plus, etc.) have >30 min.

questions.question: Mapesil LM is a NEUTRAL silicone. What does that primarily mean for the user?
options: It cures faster than acetic silicones
It does not release acetic acid, so it's safe on marble and acid-sensitive stone
It can only be used indoors
questions.correct_answer: It does not release acetic acid, so it's safe on marble and acid-sensitive stone
questions.explanation: Acetic silicones (AC, Z Plus) release acetic acid during cure, which etches limestone, marble and natural stone. Mapesil LM is neutral-curing and ASTM C1248 non-staining — designed for stone.

questions.question: What is the application temperature range of Granirapid?
questions.explanation: Granirapid: +5 to +40 °C — standard range for cementitious adhesives. +5 to +35 is Ultralite S2 only. +10 to +30 is Keralastic. +12 to +30 is Kerapoxy (narrowest).

questions.question: What is the shelf-life (storage) of Keralastic and Kerapoxy?
questions.explanation: Reaction-resin products (Keralastic, Keralastic T, Kerapoxy, Kerapoxy Adhesive) all store 24 months. Cementitious adhesives store 12 months. Mapesil LM and Z Plus store 18 months.

questions.question: What is the 28-day initial adhesion strength of Granirapid (per EN 1348)?
questions.explanation: Granirapid achieves 2.5 N/mm² at 28 days — same as Ultralite S2 and S2 Quick. 1.0 is Mapeset (basic), 1.4 is Adesilex P7 / Kerabond T, 2.0 is Keraflex Maxi S1 Zero / Ultralite S1.

questions.question: What does the 'W' stand for in Keracolor FF's CG2WA classification?
questions.explanation: W = grout with reduced water absorption (DropEffect® water-repellent technology). A = high abrasion resistance. CG2 = improved cementitious grout per EN 13888.

questions.question: What is the maximum joint width Keracolor FF can fill?
questions.explanation: Keracolor FF (the 'FF' = Fugolastic Fine / fine joints) is rated for joints up to 6 mm. For wider joints Mapei has other grouts (e.g. Ultracolor Plus).

questions.question: Which Mapei product features 'Low Dust' technology to reduce airborne dust during mixing?
questions.explanation: Ultralite S1 has Mapei's Low Dust technology — its main selling point alongside ~60% higher yield vs conventional Mapei cementitious adhesives.

questions.question: A customer is renovating a bathroom and wants to install large 60×120 cm porcelain tiles on a wall over an OLD ceramic tile floor that they don't want to remove. Which adhesive do you recommend?
options: Ultralite S2 (lightweight, S2 highly deformable, ideal for thin/large porcelain)
questions.correct_answer: Ultralite S2 (lightweight, S2 highly deformable, ideal for thin/large porcelain)
questions.explanation: Tile-on-tile + large-format porcelain on a wall = needs (a) tile-on-tile capability, (b) high deformability, (c) slip-resistance for vertical, (d) lightweight for ease. Ultralite S2 ticks all four. Mapeset and Kerabond T cap at 900 cm² for tile-on-tile and aren't deformable enough for large porcelain.

questions.question: A customer says: 'I'm building an outdoor swimming pool and need to fill it as fast as possible after tiling — what adhesive should I use?'
options: Keraflex Maxi S1 Zero (21 days before filling)
questions.explanation: Granirapid is fast-setting and explicitly rated for pools fillable after 3 days. All standard C2TE adhesives need 21 days. Note: for pools with very sensitive natural stone the answer would change to Keralastic.

questions.question: A homeowner is sealing the joint between a marble vanity top and the wall. Which sealant should you recommend, and why?
options: Mapesil AC — biggest colour range available
Mapesil Z Plus — designed for sanitary fittings
Mapesil LM — neutral cure, won't stain or etch the marble
questions.correct_answer: Mapesil LM — neutral cure, won't stain or etch the marble
questions.explanation: Acetic silicones (AC, Z Plus) release acetic acid that etches marble and stains acid-sensitive stones. Mapesil LM is neutral-curing and ASTM C1248 non-staining — the only correct choice for marble. Kerapoxy is a grout/adhesive, not a sealant.

questions.question: A restaurant client needs grout for a busy commercial kitchen — they need it acid-resistant, food-industry compliant, and easy to clean. What do you recommend?
questions.explanation: Kerapoxy is HACCP-compliant per EC Reg. 852/2004 — explicitly designed for dairies, abattoirs, breweries, food-industry environments. It's acid-resistant for most acids and easy to clean. Keracolor FF is only acid-resistant if pH > 3 and is not HACCP-rated.

questions.question: A contractor needs to bond ceramic tiles onto a metal substrate — which is the only suitable Mapei product from this set?
questions.explanation: Every cementitious adhesive in this set explicitly forbids metal. Keralastic/Keralastic T are reaction-resin polyurethanes specifically designed to bond to metal, wood, PVC, rubber, asphalt, and fibre-cement. Kerapoxy Adhesive can also bond metal but the rust must first be removed by sand-blasting — Keralastic is the cleaner, primary recommendation.

questions.question: A customer mixes Kerabond T with Isolastic latex INSTEAD of water. What is the resulting classification?
questions.explanation: This is the classic upgrade trick: Kerabond T (C1T) + Isolastic neat = C2ES2 (highly deformable improved). Kerabond Plus (C2E) gets a different upgrade — 1:1 Isolastic:water → C2ES1; neat Isolastic → C2ES2.

questions.question: Which Mapei product has the LONGEST adjustment time in the entire workbook — useful when installing complex tile patterns that need repositioning?
questions.explanation: Kerapoxy Adhesive has 130 minutes of adjustment time — longest in the set. Keralastic T comes second at 90 min. Granirapid's '3-4 h' is light foot traffic time, not adjustment time (its adjustment is ~3 h).

questions.question: For Keraflex (one-component cementitious), how does the water requirement differ between the GREY and WHITE versions per 25 kg bag?
options: Grey and white use the same water amount
Grey uses MORE water than white
White uses MORE water than grey (9.0–9.5 L vs 7.5–8.0 L)
White cannot be mixed with water — only with Isolastic
questions.correct_answer: White uses MORE water than grey (9.0–9.5 L vs 7.5–8.0 L)
questions.explanation: Keraflex white needs 9.0–9.5 L of water per 25 kg bag vs 7.5–8.0 L for grey. Adesilex P9 is the same way. This is OPPOSITE to Mapeset and Kerabond T (where white uses less water). Common trick question — most assume grey/white are identical.

questions.question: Which silicone sealant has the LOWEST movement-capability rating, making it the LEAST flexible of the three?
options: All three are rated equally at ±25%
questions.explanation: Mapesil Z Plus is rated only ±12.5% movement — half that of AC and LM. This is why Z Plus is restricted to sanitary fittings (low movement) and explicitly NOT recommended for pool expansion joints — use Mapesil AC for high-movement joints.
